trade.summary: The EAU exports refined petroleum, chemicals, and gold to Indonesia. Indonesia exports coal, palm oil, electronics, and textiles to the EAU. The EAU serves as a transit hub for Indonesian goods into the Middle East and Africa. A Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) was signed in 2022.

diplomatic.summary: Relations are warm, underpinned by shared Islamic heritage and growing economic complementarity. The CEPA signed in 2022 aims to double trade to $10B. Indonesia has a large migrant worker community in the EAU. High-level visits are frequent. Both countries align on some OIC positions.

military.summary: Limited but growing defense ties. EAU and Indonesia have engaged in defense industry discussions. Indonesia is developing its domestic defense industry and has shown interest in EAU-produced defense technology. No formal alliance.

financial.summary: Mubadala has invested in Indonesian infrastructure, nickel, and technology assets. EAU sovereign funds participated in Indonesia's $32B new capital Nusantara project. Abu Dhabi committed $10B to Indonesia's sovereign wealth fund (INA). Financial cooperation deepening rapidly.

energy.summary: Indonesia is a significant coal and LNG exporter. The EAU imports Indonesian coal for power generation and re-exports. Indonesia imports EAU refined petroleum. Cooperation on clean energy and green hydrogen discussed.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific collaboration through Islamic scholarship networks and technology partnerships. EAU and Indonesia cooperate in Islamic finance research and halal industry development. University partnerships are expanding.
